In Andhra Pradesh, the state government aggressively utilized the weekend to launch a highly publicized push for international tourism. ## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On the incredibly tragic, massive, and absolutely horrifying night of December 2-3, 1984, the absolute worst massive industrial disaster in human history aggressively and violently struck the city of Bhopal. The incredibly massive, highly negligent Union Carbide India Limited pesticide plant catastrophically leaked over 40 tons of incredibly lethal, massive methyl isocyanate gas. The incredibly massive, highly toxic cloud completely blanketed the sleeping city, aggressively resulting in the incredibly horrific, absolute immediate deaths of thousands of people and inflicting massive, generational genetic damage on hundreds of thousands of deeply traumatized survivors.

On December 2, 1911, the incredibly massive, universally iconic, and absolutely majestic architectural marvel, the Gateway of India, was officially and aggressively commemorated in Bombay (Mumbai). The incredibly massive foundation stone was laid to heavily honor the absolutely historic, massive royal visit of King George V and Queen Mary. The incredibly massive, basalt monument fundamentally and aggressively evolved into the absolute defining icon of the massive port city, heavily symbolizing both the massive zenith of the British Raj and its eventual, absolute massive departure in 1948.
